metadata.name: Sri Lanka Baseline Security Standard (BSS)
metadata.description: The Sri Lanka Baseline Security Standard provides a comprehensive framework for information security management for financial institutions regulated by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka. It establishes minimum security requirements across 14 security domains covering governance, operations, access control, and business continuity.
metadata.source_organization: Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) / Bank CSIRT
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SL-CSF is a regulatory baseline security standard issued by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka for financial institutions. It mandates specific security controls and practices for banks and financial entities operating in Sri Lanka.
metadata.framework_purpose: To establish baseline security requirements for financial institutions in Sri Lanka, ensuring the protection of information assets and maintaining the integrity of financial systems.
metadata.framework_scope: All financial institutions regulated by the Central Bank of Sri Lanka, including banks, finance companies, and other licensed financial entities.
framework_objectives: Establish information security governance and management
Implement comprehensive security policies and procedures
Protect information assets through appropriate controls
Ensure business continuity and incident response capabilities
Manage third-party and supply chain security risks
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT Security Teams, Compliance Officers, Risk Managers, and Board Members of CBSL-regulated financial institutions
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions, fines, and restrictions on business activities as determined by CBSL.
metadata.adoption_approach: Risk-based implementation with periodic self-assessments and regulatory reviews to ensure compliance with baseline security requirements.

sections: Domain 1: Organization of Information Security Management
Domain 13: Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance

controls.title: Management Commitment to Information Security
controls.description: Management shall establish a clear information security policy aligned with organizational objectives, approved by senior levels, and reviewed periodically.
controls.full_text: Management shall establish a clear information security policy aligned with business objectives. The policy must be approved by senior management and reviewed periodically to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness.

controls.title: Information Security Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessments shall include quantifying risks, determining management priorities, and periodically updating results.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall conduct comprehensive information security risk assessments that include quantifying risks, determining management priorities based on risk levels, and periodically updating assessment results to reflect changes in the threat landscape.

controls.title: Information Security Risk Treatment
controls.description: Risk treatment decisions shall be made following risk assessments.
controls.full_text: Following risk assessments, organizations shall make informed risk treatment decisions including risk acceptance, mitigation, transfer, or avoidance based on organizational risk appetite and business requirements.

controls.title: Information Security Coordination
controls.description: Coordination shall be undertaken by authorized representatives with required technical skills.
controls.full_text: Information security coordination activities shall be undertaken by authorized representatives who possess the required technical skills and organizational authority to effectively manage security across the enterprise.

controls.title: Allocation of Information Security Responsibilities
controls.description: Responsibilities shall be clearly defined and include non-disclosure agreements.
controls.full_text: Information security responsibilities shall be clearly defined and allocated to appropriate personnel. All individuals with security responsibilities must sign non-disclosure agreements acknowledging their duties.

controls.title: Communication with Authorities
controls.description: Organizations must define procedures for communicating with external authorities.
controls.full_text: Organizations must define and maintain procedures for appropriate communication with external authorities including regulators, law enforcement, and other relevant government entities regarding security matters.

controls.title: Communication with Special Interest Groups
controls.description: Maintain appropriate contacts with special information security-related groups.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall maintain appropriate contacts with special interest groups, security forums, and professional associations to stay informed about security best practices and emerging threats.

controls.title: Independent Review of Information Security
controls.description: Information security policies and procedures shall undergo periodic review.
controls.full_text: Information security policies, procedures, and their implementation shall undergo periodic independent review to ensure continued appropriateness, adequacy, and effectiveness.

controls.title: Development of Information Security Policy Document
controls.description: Define information security policy objectives and scope aligned with legal and regulatory compliance.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall develop a comprehensive information security policy document that defines security objectives and scope, aligned with applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual compliance requirements.

controls.title: Policy Review
controls.description: The policy document shall be reviewed regularly and updated as per organizational changes.
controls.full_text: The information security policy document shall be reviewed at planned intervals or when significant changes occur to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness.

controls.title: Administration and Implementation
controls.description: Administered by an information security officer ensuring adherence to IS policies.
controls.full_text: The information security policy shall be administered by a designated information security officer who is responsible for ensuring organization-wide adherence to information security policies and procedures.

controls.title: Identification of Risk Related to Third Parties
controls.description: Risk to information systems shall be identified and appropriate controls implemented.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall identify and assess risks to information and information systems from business processes involving third parties and implement appropriate controls to mitigate identified risks.

controls.title: Addressing Security in Third Party Agreements
controls.description: All information security requirements shall be covered in agreements.
controls.full_text: All relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed upon with each third party through formal agreements that may access, process, store, communicate, or provide IT infrastructure components.

controls.title: Service Delivery
controls.description: Service agreements (SLAs) must define security requirements and deliverables.
controls.full_text: Service level agreements with third parties must clearly define security requirements, performance metrics, and deliverables to ensure consistent and secure service delivery.

controls.title: Monitoring and Review of Third Party Services
controls.description: Regular review of services rendered to ensure compliance.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall regularly monitor, review, and audit third party services to ensure they are being delivered in accordance with agreed terms and security requirements.

controls.title: Inventory of Information Assets
controls.description: Maintain, identify, and recover information through inventories.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall maintain an inventory of all information assets, enabling identification, classification, and recovery of information assets when required.

controls.title: Asset Classification
controls.description: Information classification guidelines define use and required labeling.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish information classification guidelines that define the appropriate use and handling of information assets, including required labeling and protection measures.

controls.title: Acceptable Use of Information Assets
controls.description: Define and review acceptable use rules for personnel.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall define, document, and regularly review acceptable use rules for information assets to be followed by all personnel with access to organizational assets.

controls.title: Definition of Roles
controls.description: Policies shall define user responsibilities.
controls.full_text: Security policies shall clearly define user roles and responsibilities regarding information security, ensuring all personnel understand their obligations.

controls.title: Security Screening
controls.description: Screening checks are mandatory for candidates in sensitive roles.
controls.full_text: Background verification and screening checks shall be carried out for all candidates for employment, especially those applying for positions with access to sensitive information or critical systems.

controls.title: Terms and Conditions of Employment
controls.description: All employment contracts shall reference the Information Security Policy.
controls.full_text: Employment contracts shall include terms and conditions that reference the organization's information security policy, clearly stating the employee's responsibilities for information security.

controls.title: Management Responsibilities
controls.description: Brief users on roles before granting system access.
controls.full_text: Management shall ensure that all users are properly briefed on their security roles and responsibilities before being granted access to information systems.

controls.title: Information Security Awareness, Education and Training
controls.description: Regular updates and appropriate training shall be provided.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall provide regular information security awareness updates and appropriate training to all employees based on their roles and responsibilities.

controls.title: Breach of Security and Penalties
controls.description: Formal disciplinary procedure for investigation of security incidents.
controls.full_text: Organizations shall establish a formal disciplinary procedure for employees who have committed a security breach, ensuring consistent and fair handling of security violations.

controls.title: Termination Formalities
controls.description: Responsibilities for employment termination clearly defined and assigned.
controls.full_text: Responsibilities for performing employment termination or change of employment shall be clearly defined and assigned to ensure secure offboarding of personnel.

controls.title: Return of Information Assets
controls.description: All users shall return organization's information assets upon termination.
controls.full_text: All employees and third party users shall return all organizational assets in their possession upon termination of their employment, contract, or agreement.

controls.title: Revocation of Access Rights
controls.description: Access shall be removed or changed with immediate effect upon termination.
controls.full_text: Access rights of all employees and third party users to information and information processing facilities shall be removed upon termination or adjusted upon change of employment.

controls.title: Information Processing Sites
controls.description: Appropriate security perimeters shall be implemented for areas containing information.
controls.full_text: Appropriate security perimeters shall be defined and implemented to protect areas that contain information and information processing facilities.

controls.title: Equipment Maintenance
controls.description: Equipment shall be maintained with annual maintenance agreements for critical operations.
controls.full_text: Equipment shall be correctly maintained to ensure continued availability and integrity, with annual maintenance agreements in place for critical operational systems.

controls.title: Documented Operating Procedures
controls.description: Operating procedures shall be documented and maintained.
controls.full_text: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented, maintained, and made available to all users who need them.

controls.title: Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Duties shall be segregated to reduce unauthorized access and modification.
controls.full_text: Duties and areas of responsibility shall be segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized or unintentional modification or misuse of organizational assets.

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: Changes to information systems shall be attended in a controlled manner.
controls.full_text: Changes to the organization, business processes, information processing facilities, and systems that affect information security shall be controlled through formal change management procedures.

controls.title: Separation of Development, Test and Operational Facilities
controls.description: Development, test and production shall be separated.
controls.full_text: Development, testing, and operational environments shall be separated to reduce the risks of unauthorized access or changes to the operational environment.

controls.title: Capacity Management
controls.description: Use of resources shall be monitored and projected for future requirements.
controls.full_text: The use of resources shall be monitored, tuned, and projections made for future capacity requirements to ensure required system performance.

controls.title: Use of Cryptographic Controls
controls.description: Cryptographic controls shall be implemented to protect information.
controls.full_text: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information shall be developed and implemented to ensure appropriate use of cryptography.

controls.title: Key Management
controls.description: Proper key management procedures shall be established.
controls.full_text: A policy on the use, protection, and lifetime of cryptographic keys shall be developed and implemented through the full lifecycle of the keys.

controls.title: Security Perimeter
controls.description: Physical security perimeter shall be defined and implemented.
controls.full_text: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information and information processing facilities.

controls.title: Physical Entry Controls
controls.description: Entry controls shall be in place at secure areas.
controls.full_text: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed access.

controls.title: Securing Offices, Rooms and Facilities
controls.description: Security for offices and facilities shall be designed and applied.
controls.full_text: Physical security for offices, rooms, and facilities shall be designed and applied based on the sensitivity of the information and assets contained within.

controls.title: Protection from External and Environmental Threats
controls.description: Physical protection against natural or manmade disaster shall be implemented.
controls.full_text: Physical protection against natural disasters, malicious attacks, or accidents shall be designed and applied to protect information processing facilities.

controls.title: Working in Secured Areas
controls.description: Physical protection and guidelines for working in secured areas shall be provided.
controls.full_text: Physical protection and guidelines for working in secure areas shall be designed and applied to ensure the security of information in those areas.

controls.title: Public Access, Delivery and Loading Areas
controls.description: Access points shall be controlled and isolated from information processing facilities.
controls.full_text: Access points such as delivery and loading areas and other points where unauthorized persons could enter the premises shall be controlled and isolated from information processing facilities.

controls.title: Utilities
controls.description: All equipment shall be protected from power failure and other disruptions.
controls.full_text: Equipment shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities such as power, water, and telecommunications.

controls.title: Cabling Security
controls.description: Power and telecommunication cables shall be protected from interception or damage.
controls.full_text: Power and telecommunications cabling carrying data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference, or damage.

controls.title: Access Control Policy
controls.description: An access control policy shall be established, documented, and reviewed.
controls.full_text: An access control policy shall be established, documented, and reviewed based on business and information security requirements.

controls.title: User Access Management
controls.description: Formal procedures shall be in place to provide access to authorized officers.
controls.full_text: Formal user registration and de-registration procedures shall be implemented to enable assignment of access rights to authorized users.

controls.title: User Identification and Authentication
controls.description: A formal user creation and deactivation procedure shall be in place.
controls.full_text: A formal process for user identification and authentication shall be implemented, including procedures for user account creation, modification, and deactivation.

controls.title: Password Management Systems
controls.description: Password governing rules shall be established in security policies.
controls.full_text: Password management systems shall be interactive and ensure quality passwords in accordance with the organization's password policy.

controls.title: Session Timeout/Limitation of Connection Time
controls.description: Termination of active sessions is required when work is completed.
controls.full_text: Inactive sessions shall be terminated after a defined period of inactivity, and connection time restrictions shall be used to provide additional security for high-risk applications.

controls.title: Electronic Messaging and Internet
controls.description: A secure framework shall be established for email and internet use.
controls.full_text: A secure framework shall be established for electronic messaging and internet use, including appropriate controls to protect information exchanged through these channels.

controls.title: Online Transactions
controls.description: Procedures shall ensure segregation of duties for transaction authentication.
controls.full_text: Procedures and controls shall be implemented to ensure segregation of duties and appropriate authentication for online transactions to prevent fraud.

controls.title: Publicly Available Information
controls.description: Organizations shall provide correct level of information to customers.
controls.full_text: Information involved in electronic commerce passing over public networks shall be protected from fraudulent activity, contract dispute, and unauthorized disclosure and modification.

controls.title: Authorization Process for Information Systems
controls.description: Allocation and revocation of access rights using controlled mechanisms.
controls.full_text: A formal authorization process shall be defined and implemented for allocating and revoking access rights to information systems.

controls.title: Media Handling
controls.description: A secure framework for electronic and printed media use and disposal.
controls.full_text: A secure framework shall be established for the handling of electronic and printed media throughout their lifecycle, including secure disposal procedures.

controls.title: Use of System Utilities
controls.description: Use of system utilities shall be restricted to authorized officers.
controls.full_text: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

controls.title: Clear Desk and Clear Screen Policy
controls.description: Clear desk policy for printed and removable storage media.
controls.full_text: A clear desk policy for papers and removable storage media and a clear screen policy for information processing facilities shall be adopted.

controls.title: Reporting Information Security Incidents
controls.description: Information security events shall be reported through appropriate channels.
controls.full_text: Information security events shall be reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible.

controls.title: Reporting Security Weaknesses
controls.description: All employees shall report observed or suspected security weaknesses.
controls.full_text: All employees and contractors using the organization's information systems and services shall be required to note and report any observed or suspected security weaknesses.

controls.title: Responsibilities and Procedures
controls.description: Management procedures for quick, effective response to incidents.
controls.full_text: Management responsibilities and procedures shall be established to ensure a quick, effective, and orderly response to information security incidents.

controls.title: Learning from Information Security Incidents
controls.description: Mechanisms to quantify and monitor incidents shall be in place.
controls.full_text: Mechanisms shall be in place to enable the types, volumes, and costs of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored for continuous improvement.

controls.title: Collection of Evidence
controls.description: Evidence for information security incidents shall be collected and preserved.
controls.full_text: Where a follow-up action against a person or organization after an information security incident involves legal action, evidence shall be collected, retained, and presented in conformance with legal requirements.

controls.title: Security Requirements Analysis and Specification
controls.description: Security requirements shall be specified for new systems.
controls.full_text: Statements of business requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems shall specify the requirements for security controls.

controls.title: Correct Processing in Applications
controls.description: Appropriate controls for correct processing shall be incorporated.
controls.full_text: Appropriate controls shall be incorporated into applications to ensure correct processing and prevent corruption of information through processing errors or deliberate acts.

controls.title: Control of Operational Software
controls.description: Procedures shall control installation of software on operational systems.
controls.full_text: Procedures shall be in place to control the installation of software on operational systems to prevent unauthorized or untested software from being deployed.

controls.title: Audit Logs
controls.description: Audit logging of activities shall be maintained for at least one year.
controls.full_text: Audit logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults, and information security events shall be produced, retained for at least one year, and regularly reviewed.

controls.title: Monitoring System Use
controls.description: Procedures for monitoring use of information processing facilities.
controls.full_text: Procedures for monitoring use of information processing facilities shall be established to detect unauthorized information processing activities.

controls.title: Network Security Management
controls.description: Networks shall be adequately managed and controlled.
controls.full_text: Networks shall be adequately managed and controlled to protect against threats and maintain security for systems and applications using the network.

controls.title: Security of Network Services
controls.description: Security requirements for network services shall be identified.
controls.full_text: Security features, service levels, and management requirements of all network services shall be identified and included in any network services agreement.

controls.title: Security in Development and Support Processes
controls.description: Security considerations in development processes.
controls.full_text: Rules for the development of software and systems shall be established and applied to developments within the organization, including security considerations throughout the development lifecycle.

controls.title: Protection of System Test Data
controls.description: Test data shall be selected carefully and protected.
controls.full_text: Test data shall be selected carefully, protected, and controlled to prevent unauthorized access or corruption during testing activities.

controls.title: Information Leakage
controls.description: Measures shall prevent possibilities of information leakage.
controls.full_text: Measures shall be implemented to prevent possibilities of information leakage through unauthorized disclosure, whether intentional or unintentional.

controls.title: Outsourced Software Development
controls.description: Licensing agreements and compliance for outsourced development.
controls.full_text: Outsourced software development shall be supervised and monitored by the organization, with appropriate licensing agreements and compliance requirements in place.

controls.title: Technical Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Timely identification and response to technical vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems being used shall be obtained in a timely fashion, exposure evaluated, and appropriate measures taken to address the associated risk.

controls.title: Control of Technical Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Effective control measures for hardware and software.
controls.full_text: Effective control measures shall be implemented for hardware and software to address technical vulnerabilities and reduce exposure to threats.

controls.title: Preparation and Approval for BCP
controls.description: An enterprise-wide BCP framework must be established and approved.
controls.full_text: An enterprise-wide business continuity planning framework must be established and approved by senior management to ensure organizational resilience.

controls.title: Business Continuity and Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risks to business continuity must be identified and assessed.
controls.full_text: Risks to business continuity must be identified and assessed through business impact analysis and risk assessment to understand potential disruptions.

controls.title: Including Information Security in BCM Process
controls.description: A process to address information security requirements for business continuity.
controls.full_text: A process shall be developed and implemented to address information security requirements for business continuity, ensuring security is maintained during disruption and recovery.

controls.title: Developing and Implementing BCP
controls.description: Comprehensive BCPs must be created, updated, reviewed, and maintained.
controls.full_text: Comprehensive business continuity plans must be created, updated, reviewed, and maintained to ensure effective response to disruptions and continuity of critical operations.

controls.title: Testing, Maintenance, and Revision of BCP
controls.description: BCP testing and updates must be routine.
controls.full_text: Business continuity plans shall be tested, maintained, and revised routinely to ensure they remain effective and reflect current organizational requirements.
